titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 676

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=69 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:44:43.32557+00 | translation moa=70 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:44:43.340057+00

p.cl: Willelmus de Lindesey . Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis salutem .

Notum sit omnibus videntibus uel audientibus
has literas me quantum ad me spectat sicut ad dominum fundi . dedisse et hac Carta mea confirmasse . deo et sancto
Cuthberto et monachis eiusdem sancti in Dunelmo seruientibus Ecclesiam de Erceldona cum vna Carucata terre
et omnibus aliis pertinenciis suis in puram et perpetuam elemosinam . Quare volo quod iidem monachi predictam
ecclesiam et omnia adiacencia illi libere et quiete et integre possideant in perpetuum . Et multuram suam
omnino quietam habeant . quicumque predictam ecclesiam de ipsis tenuerit promisi autem quod pro posse meo hanc
elemosina ad opus et utilitatem eclesie Dunelm’ manutenebo .
William of Lindsay to all sons of Holy Mother Church sends greeting.

Let it be known to all who see or hear these letters that inasmuch as it pertains to me just as to the possessor I have given and by this my charter made firm, to God, Saint Cuthbert, and to the monks of the same saint serving God in Durham, the Church of Earlston with one ploughgate of land and all its other pertinents in pure and perpetual alms. Wherefore I will that the same monks should possess the aforesaid church and all that belongs to it so freely, quitely and wholly for ever. And they should have its multure entirely quit. And whosoever might hold the aforesaid church of them I have promised however that according to my ability I shall maintain this alms for the benefit and use of the church of Durham.
